text: A 3-year-old child presents with a painful elbow held in pronation after a traction injury. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Nursemaid's Elbow (Radial head subluxation) occurs after pulling on a child's arm.

text: Trauma patient with pelvic fractures, blood at the meatus, and a high-riding prostate. Next step?
rationale: Dx: Urethral Injury. Perform Retrograde Urethrography before any blind catheterization.

text: A newborn is cyanotic. Oxygenation does not improve significantly with 100% oxygen. Likely cause?
rationale: Dx: Transposition of the Great Vessels (TGA) presents with minimal response to oxygen.

text: A 70-year-old smoker has abdominal pain after each meal and weight loss. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Chronic Mesenteric Ischemia (Abdominal Angina) occurs in patients with vascular risk.

text: Severe back pain and a pulsatile abdominal mass in an elderly smoker. Best test?
rationale: Dx: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm (AAA). CT or Ultrasound is the gold standard.
